title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
JavaScript modules let you split code into separate files and explicitly control what is shared.

ES Modules (ESM) — modern standard:
• export — mark values/functions/classes to be shared.
• import — bring them into another file.
• Named exports — export multiple things by name.
• Default export — one per file, imported with any name.
• import() — dynamic import, returns a Promise (lazy loading).

CommonJS (CJS) — Node.js legacy:
• module.exports / exports — export.
• require() — import.

Key difference: ESM is static (analysed at compile time), CJS is dynamic (evaluated at runtime). Modern Node.js and all browsers support ESM.
